name: MouseCopyPasteSelection
description: X11-style copy-on-select for macOS. Releasing the mouse after dragging across text — or double-clicking a word — copies the selection automatically. Optional middle-click paste.
author: Cato Kolås <cato.kolas@gmail.com>

config.label: Double-click window
config.description: Maximum milliseconds between two clicks for them to count as a double-click.

config.label: Enable middle-click paste
config.description: If on, middle-clicking inside a text field pastes the clipboard at the click point.

config.label: Use private selection buffer
config.description: When on (default), copy-on-select fills a private buffer instead of the system clipboard (X11 PRIMARY model). Middle-click paste reads from this private buffer. Off = legacy shared-clipboard model.

config.label: Drag threshold
config.description: Minimum cursor movement before a drag arms the copy-on-release. Prevents tiny accidental drags from triggering a copy.

config.label: Clipboard restore delay
config.description: Milliseconds to wait after a middle-click paste before restoring the real clipboard. Only used when the private selection buffer is enabled.

config.label: Paste focus-click delay
config.description: Microseconds to wait after the synthesised focus-click before the paste keystroke.

config.label: Paste typing delay
config.description: Additional microseconds slept before the Cmd+V keystroke is issued.
